title: Add install.sh Darwin branch, launchd plist template, and --uninstall
action: Per D3, extend install.sh's OS guard (currently ~line 21: dies unless uname -s == Linux) to also accept Darwin, and its arch mapping (~lines 22-26) to add a Darwin-specific case: arm64/aarch64 maps to the aarch64-apple-darwin release target; any other arch on Darwin dies with a named error pointing to a manual cargo build --release source path, per D11 (do not attempt a prebuilt download for x86_64 Darwin - there is no such asset in the release matrix). The existing token/config/data creation logic in install.sh (env file creation with chmod 600, config.json creation) is already OS-agnostic file-handling and needs NO Darwin-specific change - it will transparently pick up the new native macOS paths once cross-platform-install-1 lands, exactly like the existing Linux logic does today. Everywhere the script currently assumes systemd (unit dir creation, the heredoc-generated herdr-go.service, systemctl --user enable/daemon-reload/linger), branch on OS: Linux keeps the exact existing systemd flow unchanged; Darwin instead writes a generated launchd plist (based on the new packaging/herdr-go.plist template you create) to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/<reverse-dns-label>.plist and loads it via launchctl bootstrap gui/$(id -u) <plist-path> (falling back to launchctl load -w for older systems if bootstrap fails), guarded so re-running does not duplicate the load. IMPORTANT - the plist must NOT read, embed, or reference the token/env file in any way: read src/config/mod.rs's ensure_web_secret() function (~lines 729-751) first - it already reads HERDR_GO_WEB_SECRET from herdr-go.env directly at process startup with zero help needed from the process launcher, exactly as it already works on Linux via systemd's EnvironmentFile=. The plist's ProgramArguments simply invoke the installed binary with --config <path-to-config.json>, nothing else - do not create an EnvironmentVariables dict, do not read the env file from the shell script, do not attempt any ReadWritePaths-style sandboxing (launchd has no such mechanism; do not describe or imply one). This eliminates the second-copy-of-the-secret risk entirely by simply not creating a second copy. The plist template's KeepAlive should use SuccessfulExit=false (restart on crash, not on clean exit) with ThrottleInterval 3, mirroring herdr-go.service's Restart=always/RestartSec=3 semantics for the process-supervision behavior only - nothing about secrets. Per D10, add an --uninstall flag: on Linux, disable+stop the systemd unit and remove its file and the installed binary; on Darwin, launchctl bootout the LaunchAgent and remove its plist and the installed binary; on both, leave config/data/token files untouched and print what was left behind.

truths: install.sh accepts Darwin as well as Linux at its OS guard, with the exact prior Linux behavior unchanged
On Darwin with arm64/aarch64, install.sh downloads the aarch64-apple-darwin release asset and installs it exactly as the Linux path does for its own targets
On Darwin with any other architecture, install.sh dies with a named, actionable error - it never attempts to download a nonexistent asset

On Darwin, install.sh writes and loads a launchd LaunchAgent plist instead of a systemd unit; the plist restarts the process on crash (KeepAlive) without ever containing the login token or any secret value
The plist's ProgramArguments contain only the binary path and --config <path> - no EnvironmentVariables dict, no reference to herdr-go.env
install.sh --uninstall removes the installed binary and the platform-appropriate service registration (systemd unit on Linux, LaunchAgent on Darwin) but never touches config/data/token files
Running the Darwin install path twice does not duplicate the LaunchAgent registration or overwrite existing config/token

artifacts.substantive: real Darwin branch with launchd plist generation, load, and --uninstall - no TODO/stub, no token/env-file handling anywhere in the plist-writing code
a real launchd plist template with ProgramArguments (binary + --config only)/KeepAlive(SuccessfulExit=false)/ThrottleInterval fields - no EnvironmentVariables key

key_links: The generated plist's ProgramArguments invoke the same installed binary path install.sh places under macOS's install prefix, with --config pointing at the same config_dir()-derived path the Rust binary itself resolves (cross-platform-install-1); the binary's own ensure_web_secret() (src/config/mod.rs) is what resolves the token at runtime, not the plist
prohibitions: Do not change the existing Linux systemd flow's behavior or output
Do not attempt to download or install an x86_64-apple-darwin asset (per D11)

Do not create an EnvironmentVariables dict in the plist, do not read herdr-go.env from install.sh's Darwin branch, do not write the token or any secret value into the plist or any file other than herdr-go.env itself
Do not describe or implement a ReadWritePaths-style sandbox for the plist - no such launchd mechanism exists
Do not remove config/data/token files on --uninstall

verify: bash -n install.sh && grep -q 'Darwin' install.sh && grep -q -- '--uninstall' install.sh && ! grep -q 'EnvironmentVariables' packaging/herdr-go.plist && ! grep -qi 'herdr-go.env' packaging/herdr-go.plist && test -f packaging/herdr-go.plist && python3 -c "import plistlib; plistlib.loads(open('packaging/herdr-go.plist','rb').read())"

trace.outcome: install.sh gains a Darwin branch (OS guard + arm64 arch map, Intel-Mac named error per D11), generates and launchctl-loads a per-user LaunchAgent, and adds --uninstall for Linux+macOS; new packaging/herdr-go.plist launchd template carries no secret. Linux systemd flow unchanged.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": "No existing automated tests cover install.sh (it is exercised by the deferred real-CI macos-14 proof step at slice level, per plan.md). Verification is structural: bash -n syntax check, contract greps, and plistlib XML validation.",
  "tests_added_or_changed": "None. This cell is a shell installer + launchd plist template; the plan assigns runtime behavior proof to the slice-level real-CI runner, not this cell's fast verify.",
  "red_failure_evidence": "Prior behavior that this change alters: the pre-edit install.sh hard-failed on macOS at its OS guard. `git show HEAD~1:install.sh | sed -n 21p` => `[[ \"$(uname -s)\" == Linux ]] || die \"the one-command service installer currently supports Linux only; ...\"` — so on Darwin the old script exited non-zero before doing anything. It also had no --uninstall flag (`git show HEAD~1:install.sh | grep -c -- --uninstall` => 0). The new version accepts Darwin and adds the flag, both confirmed by the recorded VERIFY_PASS greps.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": "packaging/herdr-go.plist is a brand-new file (no prior surface to characterize; before-state = did not exist, confirmed by `ls packaging/` showing only herdr-go.service/herdr-go-dev.service). No runtime launchctl execution evidence is captured here by design: the plan (plan.md Slice 1 verify) assigns the real launchd load/restart/uninstall roundtrip proof to the deferred macos-14 CI runner step, since no macOS host is available in this Linux execution environment.",
  "verification_run": "bash -n install.sh (clean); grep -q 'Darwin' (found); grep -q -- '--uninstall' (found); ! grep -q 'EnvironmentVariables' packaging/herdr-go.plist (absent); ! grep -qi 'herdr-go.env' packaging/herdr-go.plist (absent); test -f packaging/herdr-go.plist (present); python3 plistlib.loads parses valid. All passed -> VERIFY_PASS. Linux code path kept byte-identical inside the OS==Linux else-branch."
}
trace.verify_output: VERIFY_PASS — bash -n clean; Darwin present; --uninstall present; plist has no EnvironmentVariables and no herdr-go.env reference; plist exists and plistlib.loads parses it as valid
trace.verify_command: bash -n install.sh && grep -q 'Darwin' install.sh && grep -q -- '--uninstall' install.sh && ! grep -q 'EnvironmentVariables' packaging/herdr-go.plist && ! grep -qi 'herdr-go.env' packaging/herdr-go.plist && test -f packaging/herdr-go.plist && python3 -c "import plistlib; plistlib.loads(open('packaging/herdr-go.plist','rb').read())"
